name: FeatureCardNine
description: Scroll-based timeline feature section with two animated phone frames and centered content.
details: Use for mobile-first feature presentations with visual phone mockups. Creates a vertical scroll-based timeline where each feature takes up 100vh of height. Features are displayed with title and description in the center, flanked by two animated phone frames (left and right) that slide in and rotate on scroll. Phone frames use GSAP ScrollTrigger animations: start from off-screen with rotation (-200% left, 200% right with 45deg rotation), then animate to center position with subtle rotation tilt. Desktop phones are 20 (w-20) 2xl:25 (2xl:w-25) in width and 70vh in height. Mobile phones are 45 (w-45) in width and 100 (h-100) in height. Phones display as card with rounded-theme-capped and p-1 padding. Each phone can show either an image or video (discriminated union). Feature content displays in the center with large text (5xl md:6xl title, xl description). Section has py-20 spacing. Mobile layout stacks feature content above phone pairs vertically. Desktop layout uses sticky positioning for phones while content scrolls through. Best for 2-4 features to avoid excessive scroll height.

description.example: Everything you need to build amazing products
Connect your tools and workflows effortlessly. Our platform integrates with your existing systems.

imageSrc.note: Either imageSrc or videoSrc required for phoneOne (discriminated union)

videoSrc.note: Either imageSrc or videoSrc required for phoneOne (discriminated union)

imageAlt.note: Falls back to '[title] - Phone 1' if not provided

videoAriaLabel.note: Falls back to '[title] - Phone 1 video' if not provided

imageSrc.note: Either imageSrc or videoSrc required for phoneTwo (discriminated union)

videoSrc.note: Either imageSrc or videoSrc required for phoneTwo (discriminated union)

imageAlt.note: Falls back to '[title] - Phone 2' if not provided

videoAriaLabel.note: Falls back to '[title] - Phone 2 video' if not provided

itemRules.note: Each feature takes 100vh height. Recommend 2-4 features to avoid excessive scroll.

propsSchema.features: Array<{ id: number, title: string, description: string, buttons?: ButtonConfig[], phoneOne: ({ imageSrc: string, imageAlt?: string } | { videoSrc: string, videoAriaLabel?: string }), phoneTwo: ({ imageSrc: string, imageAlt?: string } | { videoSrc: string, videoAriaLabel?: string }) }>
propsSchema.showStepNumbers: boolean (required - controls whether step number badges display)
propsSchema.title: string
propsSchema.titleSegments?: Array<{ type: 'text', content: string } | { type: 'image', src: string, alt?: string }> - For inline images in title
propsSchema.buttons?: Array<{text: string, onClick?: () => void, href?: string}>
propsSchema.textboxLayout: 'default' | 'split' | 'split-actions' | 'split-description' | 'inline-image' (required - 'inline-image' uses titleSegments for rich text with images)
propsSchema.titleImageWrapperClassName?: string - For styling image wrapper in inline-image layout
propsSchema.titleImageClassName?: string - For styling images in inline-image layout

usageExample: <FeatureCardNine features={[{ id: 1, title: 'Seamless Integration', description: 'Connect your tools and workflows effortlessly', phoneOne: { imageSrc: '/phone1.jpg' }, phoneTwo: { imageSrc: '/phone2.jpg' } }]} showStepNumbers={true} title="Powerful Features" description="Everything you need to build amazing products" textboxLayout="default" useInvertedBackground={false} />
dont: Do not use less than 2 items
Do not use more than 4 items
